description.Title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
description.Authors@R: person("First", "Last", , "first.last@example.com", role = c("aut", "cre"))
description.Description: What the package does (one paragraph).
description.Suggests: knitr, rmarkdown, testthat (>= 3.0.0)
description.Imports:
dplyr, httr, readr, jsonlite, DT, reactable, htmltools,
plotly, rlang, config
description.Author: First Last [aut, cre]

description.Title: Tools for Working with URLs and HTTP
description.Authors@R:
c(
    person("Hadley", "Wickham", , "hadley@posit.co", role = c("aut", "cre")),
    person("Posit, PBC", role = c("cph", "fnd"))
  )
description.Description:
Useful tools for working with HTTP organised by HTTP verbs
    (GET(), POST(), etc). Configuration functions make it easy to control
    additional request components (authenticate(), add_headers() and so
    on).
description.Suggests:
covr, httpuv, jpeg, knitr, png, readr, rmarkdown, testthat
(>= 0.8.0), xml2
description.Author:
Hadley Wickham [aut, cre],
  Posit, PBC [cph, fnd]

description.Title: A Modern and Flexible Web Client for R
description.Authors@R:
c(
    person("Jeroen", "Ooms", role = c("aut", "cre"), email = "jeroenooms@gmail.com",
      comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-4035-0289")),
    person("Hadley", "Wickham", role = "ctb"),
    person("Posit Software, PBC", role = "cph"))
description.Description:
Bindings to 'libcurl' <https://curl.se/libcurl/> for performing fully
    configurable HTTP/FTP requests where responses can be processed in memory, on
    disk, or streaming via the callback or connection interfaces. Some knowledge
    of 'libcurl' is recommended; for a more-user-friendly web client see the 
    'httr2' package which builds on this package with http specific tools and logic.
description.Suggests:
spelling, testthat (>= 1.0.0), knitr, jsonlite, later,
rmarkdown, httpuv (>= 1.4.4), webutils
description.Author:
Jeroen Ooms [aut, cre] (ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4035-0289>),
  Hadley Wickham [ctb],
  Posit Software, PBC [cph]

description.Title:
Toolkit for Encryption, Signatures and Certificates Based on
OpenSSL
description.Authors@R:
c(person("Jeroen", "Ooms", role = c("aut", "cre"), email = "jeroenooms@gmail.com",
    comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-4035-0289")),
    person("Oliver", "Keyes", role = "ctb"))
description.Description:
Bindings to OpenSSL libssl and libcrypto, plus custom SSH key parsers.
    Supports RSA, DSA and EC curves P-256, P-384, P-521, and curve25519. Cryptographic
    signatures can either be created and verified manually or via x509 certificates. 
    AES can be used in cbc, ctr or gcm mode for symmetric encryption; RSA for asymmetric
    (public key) encryption or EC for Diffie Hellman. High-level envelope functions 
    combine RSA and AES for encrypting arbitrary sized data. Other utilities include key
    generators, hash functions (md5, sha1, sha256, etc), base64 encoder, a secure random
    number generator, and 'bignum' math methods for manually performing crypto 
    calculations on large multibyte integers.
description.Suggests:
curl, testthat (>= 2.1.0), digest, knitr, rmarkdown,
jsonlite, jose, sodium
description.Author:
Jeroen Ooms [aut, cre] (ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4035-0289>),
  Oliver Keyes [ctb]

description.Title: Powerful and Reliable Tools for Running System Commands in R
description.Authors@R:
c(person("Jeroen", "Ooms", role = c("aut", "cre"), 
      email = "jeroenooms@gmail.com", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-4035-0289")),
    person("Gábor", "Csárdi", , "csardi.gabor@gmail.com", role = "ctb"))
description.Description:
Drop-in replacements for the base system2() function with fine control
    and consistent behavior across platforms. Supports clean interruption, timeout, 
    background tasks, and streaming STDIN / STDOUT / STDERR over binary or text 
    connections. Arguments on Windows automatically get encoded and quoted to work 
    on different locales.
description.Suggests: unix (>= 1.4), spelling, testthat
description.Author:
Jeroen Ooms [aut, cre] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4035-0289>),
  Gábor Csárdi [ctb]

description.Title: Password Entry Utilities for R, Git, and SSH
description.Authors@R:
person("Jeroen", "Ooms", role = c("aut", "cre"), 
    email = "jeroenooms@gmail.com", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-4035-0289"))
description.Description:
Cross-platform utilities for prompting the user for credentials or a 
    passphrase, for example to authenticate with a server or read a protected key.
    Includes native programs for MacOS and Windows, hence no 'tcltk' is required. 
    Password entry can be invoked in two different ways: directly from R via the 
    askpass() function, or indirectly as password-entry back-end for 'ssh-agent' 
    or 'git-credential' via the SSH_ASKPASS and GIT_ASKPASS environment variables.
    Thereby the user can be prompted for credentials or a passphrase if needed 
    when R calls out to git or ssh.
description.Suggests: testthat
description.Author: Jeroen Ooms [aut, cre] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4035-0289>)

description.Title: Encapsulated Classes with Reference Semantics
description.Authors@R:
c(
    person("Winston", "Chang", , "winston@posit.co", role = c("aut", "cre")),
    person("Posit Software, PBC", role = c("cph", "fnd"))
  )
description.Description:
Creates classes with reference semantics, similar to R's
    built-in reference classes. Compared to reference classes, R6 classes
    are simpler and lighter-weight, and they are not built on S4 classes
    so they do not require the methods package. These classes allow public
    and private members, and they support inheritance, even when the
    classes are defined in different packages.
description.Suggests: lobstr, testthat (>= 3.0.0)
description.Author:
Winston Chang [aut, cre],
  Posit Software, PBC [cph, fnd]

description.Title: Helpers for Developing Command Line Interfaces
description.Authors@R:
c(
    person("Gábor", "Csárdi", , "gabor@posit.co", role = c("aut", "cre")),
    person("Hadley", "Wickham", role = "ctb"),
    person("Kirill", "Müller", role = "ctb"),
    person("Salim", "Brüggemann", , "salim-b@pm.me", role = "ctb",
           comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-5329-5987")),
    person("Posit Software, PBC", role = c("cph", "fnd"))
  )
description.Description:
A suite of tools to build attractive command line interfaces
    ('CLIs'), from semantic elements: headings, lists, alerts, paragraphs,
    etc. Supports custom themes via a 'CSS'-like language. It also
    contains a number of lower level 'CLI' elements: rules, boxes, trees,
    and 'Unicode' symbols with 'ASCII' alternatives. It support ANSI
    colors and text styles as well.
description.Suggests:
callr, covr, crayon, digest, glue (>= 1.6.0), grDevices,
htmltools, htmlwidgets, knitr, methods, processx, ps (>=
1.3.4.9000), rlang (>= 1.0.2.9003), rmarkdown, rprojroot,
rstudioapi, testthat (>= 3.2.0), tibble, whoami, withr
description.Config/Needs/website:

fansi, prettyunits, sessioninfo, tidyverse/tidytemplate,
usethis, vctrs
description.Author:

  Hadley Wickham [ctb],
  Kirill Müller [ctb],
  Salim Brüggemann [ctb] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0002-5329-5987>),
  Posit Software, PBC [cph, fnd]

Defines new notions of prototype and size that are used to
    provide tools for consistent and well-founded type-coercion and
    size-recycling, and are in turn connected to ideas of type- and
    size-stability useful for analysing function interfaces.

description.Title: Create Compact Hash Digests of R Objects
description.Description:
Implementation of a function 'digest()' for the creation of hash
 digests of arbitrary R objects (using the 'md5', 'sha-1', 'sha-256', 'crc32',
 'xxhash', 'murmurhash', 'spookyhash', 'blake3', 'crc32c', 'xxh3_64', and 'xxh3_128'
 algorithms) permitting easy comparison of R language objects, as well as functions
 such as'hmac()' to create hash-based message authentication code. Please note that
 this package is not meant to be deployed for cryptographic purposes for which more
 comprehensive (and widely tested) libraries such as 'OpenSSL' should be used.
description.Suggests: tinytest, simplermarkdown

description.Title: A Simple and Robust JSON Parser and Generator for R
description.Authors@R:
c(
    person("Jeroen", "Ooms", role = c("aut", "cre"), email = "jeroenooms@gmail.com",
      comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-4035-0289")),
    person("Duncan", "Temple Lang", role = "ctb"),
    person("Lloyd", "Hilaiel", role = "cph", comment="author of bundled libyajl"))
description.Description:
A reasonably fast JSON parser and generator, optimized for statistical 
    data and the web. Offers simple, flexible tools for working with JSON in R, and
    is particularly powerful for building pipelines and interacting with a web API. 
    The implementation is based on the mapping described in the vignette (Ooms, 2014).
    In addition to converting JSON data from/to R objects, 'jsonlite' contains 
    functions to stream, validate, and prettify JSON data. The unit tests included 
    with the package verify that all edge cases are encoded and decoded consistently 
    for use with dynamic data in systems and applications.
description.Suggests: httr, vctrs, testthat, knitr, rmarkdown, R.rsp, sf
description.Author:
Jeroen Ooms [aut, cre] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4035-0289>),
  Duncan Temple Lang [ctb],
  Lloyd Hilaiel [cph] (author of bundled libyajl)

Guesses the MIME type from a filename extension using the data

description.Author:
Yihui Xie [aut, cre] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0645-5666>,

  Jeffrey Horner [ctb],
  Beilei Bian [ctb]

description.Description:
An implementation of interpreted string literals, inspired by
    Python's Literal String Interpolation
    <https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0498/> and Docstrings
    <https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0257/> and Julia's Triple-Quoted
    String Literals
    <https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1.3/manual/strings/#Triple-Quoted-String-Literals-1>.
description.Suggests:
crayon, DBI (>= 1.2.0), dplyr, knitr, magrittr, rlang,
rmarkdown, RSQLite, testthat (>= 3.2.0), vctrs (>= 0.3.0),
waldo (>= 0.5.3), withr
description.Config/Needs/website:
bench, forcats, ggbeeswarm, ggplot2, R.utils,
rprintf, tidyr, tidyverse/tidytemplate
description.Author:
Jim Hester [aut] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2739-7082>),
  Jennifer Bryan [aut, cre] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6983-2759>),
  Posit Software, PBC [cph, fnd]

description.Description:
Provides a 'tbl_df' class (the 'tibble') with stricter checking and better formatting than the traditional
    data frame.
description.Imports:
cli, lifecycle (>= 1.0.0), magrittr, methods, pillar (>=
1.8.1), pkgconfig, rlang (>= 1.0.2), utils, vctrs (>= 0.5.0)
description.Suggests:
bench, bit64, blob, brio, callr, DiagrammeR, dplyr, evaluate,
formattable, ggplot2, here, hms, htmltools, knitr, lubridate,
nycflights13, pkgload, purrr, rmarkdown, stringi, testthat (>=
3.0.2), tidyr, withr
description.Author:
Kirill Müller [aut, cre] (ORCID:
    <https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1416-3412>),
  Hadley Wickham [aut],
  Romain Francois [ctb],
  Jennifer Bryan [ctb],
  RStudio [cph, fnd]

description.Title: Coloured Formatting for Columns
description.Authors@R:
c(person(given = "Kirill",
             family = "M\u00fcller",

             email = "kirill@cynkra.com",
             comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-1416-3412")),
      person(given = "Hadley",

description.Description:
Provides 'pillar' and 'colonnade' generics designed
    for formatting columns of data using the full range of colours
    provided by modern terminals.
description.Imports:
cli (>= 2.3.0), glue, lifecycle, rlang (>= 1.0.2), utf8 (>=
1.1.0), utils, vctrs (>= 0.5.0)
description.Suggests:
bit64, DBI, debugme, DiagrammeR, dplyr, formattable, ggplot2,
knitr, lubridate, nanotime, nycflights13, palmerpenguins,
rmarkdown, scales, stringi, survival, testthat (>= 3.1.1),
tibble, units (>= 0.7.2), vdiffr, withr
description.Config/testthat/start-first:
format_multi_fuzz, format_multi_fuzz_2,
format_multi, ctl_colonnade, ctl_colonnade_1, ctl_colonnade_2
description.Author:
Kirill Müller [aut, cre] (ORCID:
    <https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1416-3412>),
  Hadley Wickham [aut],
  RStudio [cph]

description.Title: Private Configuration for 'R' Packages
description.Author: Gábor Csárdi
description.Description:
Set configuration options on a per-package basis.
    Options set by a given package only apply to that package,
    other packages are unaffected.
description.Suggests: covr, testthat, disposables (>= 1.0.3)

description.Title: Unicode Text Processing
description.Authors@R:
c(person(given = c("Patrick", "O."),
             family = "Perry",
             role = c("aut", "cph")),
      person(given = "Kirill",
             family = "M\u00fcller",
             role = "cre",
             email = "kirill@cynkra.com",
             comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-1416-3412")),
      person(given = "Unicode, Inc.",
             role = c("cph", "dtc"),
             comment = "Unicode Character Database"))
description.Description:
Process and print 'UTF-8' encoded international
    text (Unicode). Input, validate, normalize, encode, format, and
    display.
description.Suggests:
cli, covr, knitr, rlang, rmarkdown, testthat (>= 3.0.0),
withr
description.Author:
Patrick O. Perry [aut, cph],
  Kirill Müller [cre] (ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1416-3412>),
  Unicode, Inc. [cph, dtc] (Unicode Character Database)

description.Title: A C++11 Interface for R's C Interface
description.Authors@R:
c(
      person("Davis", "Vaughan", email = "davis@posit.co", role = c("aut", "cre"), comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0003-4777-038X")),
      person("Jim","Hester", role = "aut", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-2739-7082")),
      person("Romain", "François", role = "aut", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-2444-4226")),
      person("Benjamin", "Kietzman", role = "ctb"),
      person("Posit Software, PBC", role = c("cph", "fnd"))
    )
description.Description:
Provides a header only, C++11 interface to R's C
    interface.  Compared to other approaches 'cpp11' strives to be safe
    against long jumps from the C API as well as C++ exceptions, conform
    to normal R function semantics and supports interaction with 'ALTREP'
    vectors.
description.Suggests:
bench, brio, callr, cli, covr, decor, desc, ggplot2, glue,
knitr, lobstr, mockery, progress, rmarkdown, scales, Rcpp,
testthat (>= 3.2.0), tibble, utils, vctrs, withr
description.Config/Needs/cpp11/cpp_register:
brio, cli, decor, desc, glue, tibble,
vctrs
description.Author:
Davis Vaughan [aut, cre] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0003-4777-038X>),
  Jim Hester [aut] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2739-7082>),
  Romain François [aut] (<https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2444-4226>),
  Benjamin Kietzman [ctb],
  Posit Software, PBC [cph, fnd]
